front: Ele tentou dar uma de João sem braço, mas todo mundo sabia que ele era o responsável.
pronunciation: eh-lee tehn-TOH dahr OO-mah jee zhoh-ONG sehng BRAH-soo, mahz TOH-doh MOON-doo sah-BEE-ah keh eh-lee EH-rah oh rres-pohn-SAH-vehl.
back: He tried to play dumb, but everyone knew he was the one responsible.
context: Using 'dar uma de João sem braço' to describe someone pretending to be clueless or avoiding responsibility.

front: Não faça tempestade em copo d'água; esse atraso de cinco minutos é fácil de resolver.
pronunciation: NOHNG FAH-sah tehm-pes-TAH-jee ehng KOH-poo DAH-gwah; EHS-see ah-TRAH-zoo jee SEENG-koo mee-NOO-tooz eh FAH-seeo jee rreh-sohl-VEHR.
back: Don't make a mountain out of a molehill; this five-minute delay is easy to resolve.
context: Telling someone not to overreact to a minor issue ('tempestade em copo d'água' = storm in a teacup).

front: Você poderia quebrar esse galho para mim e entregar este documento hoje?
pronunciation: voh-SEH poh-deh-REE-ah kweh-BRAHR EHS-see GAH-lyoo PAH-rah meeng ee een-treh-GAHR EHS-tee doh-koo-MEHN-too HOY-zhee?
back: Could you do me a quick favor and deliver this document today?
context: Using 'quebrar um galho' to ask for a favor or a temporary workaround/improvised help.

front: Depois de uma semana tão estressante, decidi chutar o balde e viajar no fim de semana.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee OO-mah seh-MAH-nah TOHNG ees-tres-AHN-tee, jee-see-JEE shoo-TAHR oh BAHL-jee ee vyah-ZHAHR noo feeng jee seh-MAH-nah.
back: After such a stressful week, I decided to throw caution to the wind and travel over the weekend.
context: Using 'chutar o balde' (kick the bucket/trash can) to mean giving up control, losing one's temper, or letting loose.

front: A situação na empresa está tão tensa que todos estão pisando em ovos.
pronunciation: ah see-too-ah-ZOHNG nah ehm-PREH-zah ees-TAH TOHNG TEHN-sah keh TOH-doohz ees-TOHNG pee-ZAHN-doh ehng OH-voohz.
back: The situation at the company is so tense that everyone is walking on eggshells.
context: Describing an environment where people must act with extreme caution to avoid conflict.

front: Às vezes, no ambiente de trabalho, precisamos engolir sapo para evitar conflitos maiores.
pronunciation: ahz VEH-zeez, noo am-bee-EHN-tee jee trah-BAH-lyoo, pree-see-ZAH-mohz eheng-goh-LEER SAH-poo PAH-rah ee-vee-TAHR kohn-FLEE-toohz mah-YOH-reez.
back: Sometimes, in the workplace, we need to swallow our pride and accept unpleasant things to avoid bigger conflicts.
context: Using 'engolir sapo' (to swallow a toad) to mean tolerating unfair or offensive situations silently.

front: Fiquei com a pulga atrás da orelha quando ele mudou de assunto repentinamente.
pronunciation: fee-KAY kohm ah POOL-gah ah-TRAHZ dah oh-REHL-yah KWAHN-doh eh-lee moo-DOH jee ah-SOON-too rreh-pehn-tee-nah-MEHN-tee.
back: I became suspicious when he suddenly changed the subject.
context: Using 'com a pulga atrás da orelha' (with a flea behind the ear) to express suspicion or wariness.

front: Pode tirar o cavalinho da chuva se você acha que eu vou fazer todo o trabalho sozinho.
pronunciation: POH-jee tee-RAHR oh kah-vah-LEENG-yoo dah SHOO-vah see voh-SEH AH-shah keh_oo VOY fah-ZEHR TOH-doh oh trah-BAH-lyoo soh-ZEENG-yoo.
back: You can give up on that idea if you think I am going to do all the work by myself.
context: Using 'tirar o cavalinho da chuva' to tell someone to abandon their unrealistic expectations.

front: Não podemos ficar de braços cruzados enquanto o desmatamento continua aumentando.
pronunciation: NOHNG poh-DEH-mohz fee-KAHR jee BRAH-sooz kroo-ZAH-doohz eheng-KWAHN-too oh dez-mah-tah-MEHN-too kohn-tee-NWAH_ow-mehn-TAHN-doh.
back: We cannot stand idly by while deforestation continues to increase.
context: Using 'ficar de braços cruzados' (to stand with folded arms) to mean remaining passive or doing nothing.

front: Ele cometeu o erro, mas fui eu quem acabou pagando o pato perante a diretoria.
pronunciation: EH-lee koh-meh-TEW oh EH-rroo, mahz fwee_oo KEHNG ah-kah-BOH pah-GAHN-doh oh PAH-too peh-RAHN-tee ah jee-reh-toh-REE-ah.
back: He made the mistake, but I was the one who ended up taking the blame in front of the board.
context: Using 'pagar o pato' (to pay for the duck) to mean taking the fall or suffering consequences for others.

front: Minha vizinha é muito simpática, mas quando começa a conversar, fala pelos cotovelos.
pronunciation: MEENG-yah vee-ZEENG-yah eh MWEE-too seem-PAH-tee-kah, mahz KWAHN-doh koh-MEH-sah ah kohn-vehr-SAHR, FAH-lah PEH-loohz koh-toh-VEH-loohz.
back: My neighbor is very nice, but when she starts talking, she talks a blue streak.
context: Using 'falar pelos cotovelos' (to talk through one's elbows) to describe someone who talks excessively.

front: No casamento da minha prima, todo mundo enfiou o pé na jaca na hora do doce.
pronunciation: noo kah-zah-MEHN-toh dah MEENG-yah PREE-mah, TOH-doh MOON-doo eheng-fee-OH oh PEH nah ZHAH-kah nah OH-rah doh DOH-see.
back: At my cousin's wedding, everyone overindulged when it was time for dessert.
context: Using 'enfiar o pé na jaca' (to stick one's foot in the jackfruit) to mean overindulging, overeating, or drinking too much.

front: Demorou um tempo, mas finalmente caiu a ficha de que eu tinha passado na prova.
pronunciation: deh-moh-ROH oong TEHM-poo, mahz fee-nahl-MEHN-tee kah-EE_ah FEE-shah jee keh_oo TEENG-yah pah-SAH-doh nah PROH-vah.
back: It took a while, but the penny finally dropped that I had passed the exam.
context: Using 'cair a ficha' (the coin/token falling) to mean finally realizing or understanding something.

front: As crianças estão com a corda toda hoje; não param de correr pela casa um segundo.
pronunciation: ahz kree-AHN-sahz ees-TOHNG kohm ah KOHR-dah TOH-dah HOY-zhee; NOHNG PAH-rahm jee kohr-REHR PEH-lah KAH-sah oong seh-GOON-doo.
back: The kids are full of energy today; they don't stop running around the house for a second.
context: Using 'estar com a corda toda' (to be with the full rope) to mean being highly energetic or excited.

front: Pedi para ele guardar segredo, mas ele deu com a língua nos dentes quase imediatamente.
pronunciation: peh-JEE PAH-rah EH-lee gwahr-DAHR seh-GREH-doh, mahz EH-lee DEW kohm ah LEENG-gwah noohz DEHN-teez KWAH-zee ee-meh-jah-tah-MEHN-tee.
back: I asked him to keep it a secret, but he spilled the beans almost immediately.
context: Using 'dar com a língua nos dentes' (to strike the teeth with the tongue) to mean letting a secret slip.

front: Detesto sair com casais apaixonados para não ter que ficar segurando vela.
pronunciation: deh-TEHS-too sah-EER kohm kah-ZAHEEZ ah-pay-shoh-NAH-doohz PAH-rah NOHNG TEHR keh fee-KAHR seh-goo-RAHN-doh VEH-lah.
back: I hate going out with couples in love so I don't have to be a third wheel.
context: Using 'segurar vela' (to hold a candle) to describe being the awkward third wheel in a romantic setting.

front: O chefe resolveu fazer vista grossa para o pequeno atraso devido ao trânsito caótico.
pronunciation: oh SHEH-fee rreh-sohl-VEW fah-ZEHR VEES-tah GROHS-sah PAH-rah oh peh-KEH-noo ah-TRAH-zoo deh-VEE-doh AH-oo TRAN-zee-too kah-OH-jee-koh.
back: The boss decided to turn a blind eye to the small delay due to the chaotic traffic.
context: Using 'fazer vista grossa' (to make a thick eye/sight) to mean intentionally ignoring an infraction or error.

front: Aquele pobre homem vive sem eira nem beira pelas ruas do centro histórico.
pronunciation: ah-KEH-lee POH-bree OH-meheng VEE-vee sehng AY-rah neheng BAY-rah PEH-lahz RROO-ahz doh SEHN-troo ees-TOH-ree-koh.
back: That poor man lives with no place to call home wandering the streets of the historic center.
context: Using 'sem eira nem beira' (without courtyard or edge) to describe someone destitute, homeless, or completely broke.

front: Nosso planejamento para as férias foi para o vinagre por causa da tempestade.
pronunciation: NOHS-sooh plah-neh-zhah-MEHN-toh PAH-rah az FAY-ryahz FOY PAH-rah oh vee-NAH-gree pohr KOW-zah dah tehm-pes-TAH-jee.
back: Our vacation plans went down the drain because of the storm.
context: Using 'ir para o vinagre' (to go to vinegar) to mean a plan failing or turning sour completely.

front: Fique atento durante a reunião para não comer mosca e perder prazos cruciais.
pronunciation: FEE-kee ah-TEHN-too doo-RAHN-tee ah rrehoo-nee-ONG PAH-rah NOHNG koh-MEHR MOHS-kah ee pehr-DEHR PRAH-zoohz kroo-SYAHEEZ.
back: Stay sharp during the meeting so you don't miss something obvious and lose crucial deadlines.
context: Using 'comer mosca' (to eat a fly) to mean missing an obvious point, being distracted, or making a silly mistake.

front: Você está viajando na maionese se acha que esse projeto vai ficar pronto amanhã.
pronunciation: voh-SEH ees-TAH vyah-ZHAHN-doh nah mah-yo-NEH-zee see AH-shah keh EHS-see proh-ZHEH-toh VAY fee-KAHR PROHN-too ah-mhang-YAH.
back: You are completely daydreaming if you think this project will be ready tomorrow.
context: Using 'viajar na maionese' (to travel in mayonnaise) to mean talking nonsense, imagining things, or being detached from reality.

front: O jogador veterano de futebol decidiu que de fato era hora de pendurar as chuteiras.
pronunciation: oh zhoh-gah-DOHR veh-teh-RAH-no jee foo-chee-BOHL jee-see-JEE keh jee FAH-too EH-rah OH-rah jee pehn-doo-RAHR ahz shoo-TAY-rahz.
back: The veteran soccer player decided that indeed it was time to hang up his boots.
context: Using 'pendurar as chuteiras' (to hang up the soccer shoes) as a metaphor for retirement in sports or professional life.

front: Pare de procurar pelo em ovo e apenas aceite que o relatório está perfeito.
pronunciation: PAH-ree jee proh-koo-RAHR PEH-loo ehng OH-voo ee ah-PEH-nahz ah-SAY-chee keh oh rreh-lah-TOH-ryoo ees-TAH pehr-FAY-too.
back: Stop nitpicking and just accept that the report is perfect.
context: Using 'procurar pelo em ovo' (to look for hair on an egg) to describe nitpicking or finding nonexistent problems.

front: Depois de ver a demissão inesperada do colega, resolvi colocar as barbas de molho.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee VEHR ah deh-mee-SOHNG ee-nes-peh-RAH-dah doh koh-LEH-gah, rreh-sohl-VEE koh-loh-KAHR ahz BAHR-bahz jee MOH-lyoo.
back: After seeing the colleague's unexpected dismissal, I decided to watch my step and be cautious.
context: Using 'colocar as barbas de molho' (to put one's beard to soak) to mean being cautious in a risky situation.

front: Fiquei tão nervoso durante a apresentação que acabei trocando as bolas com os nomes.
pronunciation: fee-KAY TOHNG nehr-VOH-zoo doo-RAHN-tee ah ah-preh-zehn-tah-ZOHNG keh ah-kah-BAY troh-KAHN-doh ahz BOH-lahz kohm ooz NOH-meez.
back: I got so nervous during the presentation that I ended up mixing up the names.
context: Using 'trocar as bolas' (to swap the balls) to mean getting confused or mixing things up.

front: Sobre esse assunto polêmico, prefiro lavar as mãos e não tomar nenhum partido.
pronunciation: SOH-bree EHS-see ah-SOON-too poh-LEH-mee-koh, preh-FEE-roo lah-VAHR ahz MAHEEZ ee NOHNG toh-MAHR neheng-GOONG pahr-TEE-doh.
back: Regarding this controversial subject, I prefer to wash my hands of it and not take any side.
context: Using 'lavar as mãos' (to wash one's hands) to denote avoiding responsibility or refusing to intervene.

front: Na pista de dança da festa, ela finalmente se soltou e resolveu soltar a franga.
pronunciation: nah PEES-tah jee DAN-sah dah FES-tah, EH-lyah fee-nahl-MEHN-tee see sohl-TOH ee rreh-sohl-VEW sohl-TAHR ah FRAHN-gah.
back: On the party's dance floor, she finally let loose and decided to go wild.
context: Using 'soltar a franga' (to release the chicken) to mean letting down all inhibitions and having fun.

front: Com essa nova proposta em mãos, estamos com a faca e o queijo na mão para fechar o negócio.
pronunciation: kohm EHS-sah NOH-vah proh-POHS-tah ehng MAHEEZ, ees-TAH-mohz kohm ah FAH-kah ee oh KAY-zhoo nah MONG PAH-rah feh-SHAHR oh neh-GOH-syoo.
back: With this new proposal in hand, we have everything we need to seal the deal.
context: Using 'com a faca e o queijo na mão' (with the knife and cheese in hand) to mean having all the control or leverage.

front: Tentar convencer aquele teimoso é o mesmo que dar murro em ponta de faca.
pronunciation: tehn-TAHR kohn-vehn-SEHR ah-KEH-lee tay-MOH-zoo eh oh MEHS-moo keh dahr MOO-rroo ehng POHN-tah jee FAH-kah.
back: Trying to convince that stubborn guy is the same as beating your head against a brick wall.
context: Using 'dar murro em ponta de faca' (to punch a knife point) to describe a painful, futile struggle.

front: Se o sistema de vendas falhar novamente hoje, nossa equipe inteira vai entrar pelo cano.
pronunciation: see oh sees-TEH-mah jee VEHN-dahz fahl-YAHR noh-vah-MEHN-tee HOY-zhee, NOHS-sah ee-KEE-pee een-TAY-rah VAY ehn-TRAHR PEH-loo KAH-noo.
back: If the sales system fails again today, our entire team will go down the tubes.
context: Using 'entrar pelo cano' (to go through the pipe) to mean getting into trouble or failing miserably.

front: Sem internet e com o prazo se esgotando, nos vimos em um mato sem cachorro.
pronunciation: sehng een-tehr-NEHT ee kohm oh PRAH-zoo see ees-goh-TAHN-doh, nohz VEE-mohz ehng oong MAH-too sehng kah-SHOH-rroo.
back: Without internet and with the deadline running out, we found ourselves in a tight spot with no options.
context: Using 'estar no mato sem cachorro' (to be in the woods without a dog) to indicate being in a tough jam.

front: Não quero botar lenha na fogueira, mas ouvi dizer que o orçamento será reduzido.
pronunciation: NOHNG KEH-roo boh-TAHR LAY-nyah nah foh-GAY-rah, mahz oh-VEE jee-ZEER keh oh ohr-sah-MEHN-toh seh-RAH rreh-doo-ZEE-doh.
back: I don't want to add fuel to the fire, but I heard that the budget will be reduced.
context: Using 'botar lenha na fogueira' (to put wood on the bonfire) to mean escalating a delicate situation.

front: Depois de tudo que ele fez de errado, ainda quer aumento? Como dormir com um barulho desses?
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee TOH-doh keh EH-lee FEHZ jee eh-RRAH-doh, ah-EENG-dah KEHR ow-MEHN-toh? KOH-moo doohr-MEER kohm oong bah-ROO-lyoo DEHS-seez?
back: After everything he did wrong, he still wants a raise? How can anyone make sense of that?
context: Using 'como dormir com um barulho desse' (how to sleep with such noise) to express disbelief at an absurd situation.

front: Aquele sujeito é tão cara de pau que pediu dinheiro emprestado logo após me ofender.
pronunciation: ah-KEH-lee soo-ZHAY-too eh TOHNG KAH-rah jee PAW keh peh-ZHEW jee-NYAY-roo eheng-pres-TAH-doh LOH-goo AH-pohz mee oh-fehn-DEHR.
back: That guy is so shameless that he asked to borrow money right after offending me.
context: Using 'cara de pau' (wood face) to describe someone extremely shameless, cheeky, or bold.

front: Já estou de saco cheio de receber ligações de telemarketing o dia inteiro.
pronunciation: ZHAH ees-TOY jee SAH-koo SHAY-oo jee rreh-seh-BEHR lee-gah-SOHNGZ jee teh-leh-MAHR-keh-teeng oh DEE-ah een-TAY-roo.
back: I'm already fed up with receiving telemarketing calls all day long.
context: Using 'de saco cheio' (full sack/bag) to say you have absolutely had enough of a situation.

front: Ele apenas deu de ombros quando lhe mostrei o erro evidente no código.
pronunciation: EH-lee ah-PEH-nahz DEW jee OHM-broohz KWAHN-doh lee mohs-TRAY oh EH-rroo ee-vee-DEHN-tee noo KOH-jee-goo.
back: He just shrugged his shoulders when I showed him the obvious error in the code.
context: Using 'dar de ombros' to mean shrugging, showing indifference or lack of concern.

front: Ela prometeu me ajudar com a mudança, mas na hora H, acabou me deixando na mão.
pronunciation: EH-lyah proh-meh-TEW mee ah-zhoo-DAHR kohm ah moo-DAN-sah, mahz nah OH-rah ah-gah, ah-kah-BOH mee day-SHAHN-doh nah MONG.
back: She promised to help me with the move, but at the last minute, she ended up letting me down.
context: Using 'na hora H' (at hour H / crunch time) and 'deixar na mão' (to leave stranded/in the lurch).

front: Não vá com muita sede ao pote para não assustar os novos investidores.
pronunciation: NOHNG VAH kohm MWEE-tah SEH-jee AH-oo POH-chee PAH-rah NOHNG ah-soos-TAHR ohz NOH-voohz een-ves-chee-DOH-rez.
back: Don't go in with too much eagerness so you don't scare off the new investors.
context: Using 'ir com muita sede ao pote' (to go with too much thirst to the pot) to warn against overeagerness.

front: Esse sistema de software antigo é um verdadeiro elefante branco na empresa.
pronunciation: EHS-see sees-TEH-mah jee SOHFT-wehr ahn-TEE-goo eh oong vehr-dah-DAY-roo eh-leh-FAHN-tee BRAHN-koo nah ehm-PREH-zah.
back: This ancient software system is a true white elephant in the company.
context: Using 'elefante branco' to describe a costly, high-maintenance possession with little real utility.

front: Precisamos separar o joio do trigo antes de decidir quem será promovido.
pronunciation: pree-see-ZAH-mohz seh-pah-RAHR oh ZHOY-oo doh TREE-goo AHN-teez jee jee-see-DEER KEHNG seh-RAH proh-moh-VEE-doh.
back: We need to separate the wheat from the chaff before deciding who will be promoted.
context: Using 'separar o joio do trigo' to differentiate between what is valuable and what is worthless.

front: Ele vive chorando as pitangas por causa do salário, mas não procura outro emprego.
pronunciation: EH-lee VEE-vee shoh-RAHN-doh ahz pee-TAHN-gahz pohr KOW-zah doh sah-LAH-ryoo, mahz NOHNG proh-KOO-rah OW-troo ehm-PREH-goo.
back: He's always whining about his salary, but he doesn't look for another job.
context: Using 'chorar as pitangas' (to cry Surinam cherries) to mean continuously whining, complaining, or venting.

front: Não adianta chorar sobre o leite derramado; agora temos que focar na solução.
pronunciation: NOHNG ah-jee-AHN-tah shoh-RAHR SOH-bree oh LAY-chee dehr-rah-MAH-doh; ah-GOH-rah TEH-mohz keh foh-KAHR nah soh-loo-ZOHNG.
back: It is no use crying over spilled milk; now we have to focus on the solution.
context: A classic idiom meaning it is useless to regret things that have already happened.

front: Depois de muito esforço, finalmente consegui matar dois coelhos com uma cajadada só.
pronunciation: deh-POY_Z jee MWEE-too ees-FOHR-soo, fee-nahl-MEHN-tee kohn-seh-GHEE mah-TAHR doyz koh-EH-lyoohz kohm OO-mah kah-zhah-DAH-dah SOH.
back: After a lot of effort, I finally managed to kill two birds with one stone.
context: Using 'matar dois coelhos com uma cajadada só' (to kill two rabbits with one cane strike) to hit two goals at once.

front: Ela sempre tenta tapar o sol com a peneira fingindo que a empresa não tem problemas econômicos.
pronunciation: EH-lyah SEHM-pree TEHN-tah tah-PAHR oh SOHL kohm ah peh-NAY-rah feeng-ZHEHN-doh keh ah ehm-PREH-zah NOHNG teheng proh-BLEH-mahz eh-koh-NOH-mee-koohz.
back: She always tries to cover up the cracks by pretending the company has no economic problems.
context: Using 'tapar o sol com a peneira' (to block the sun with a sieve) to mean using a weak, ineffective cover-up.

front: Ele pensou que ia nos enganar com facilidade, mas acabou caindo do cavalo.
pronunciation: EH-lee pehn-SOH keh EE-ah nohz eheng-gah-NAHR kohm fah-see-lee-DAH-jee, mahz ah-kah-BOH kah-EENG-doh doh kah-VAH-loo.
back: He thought he was going to deceive us easily, but he ended up taking a hard fall.
context: Using 'cair do cavalo' (to fall off the horse) to denote experiencing a sudden failure or rude awakening.
